name: Albero Alto and Peña Mediodía Loop
name_original: Valle del Río Flumen y Peña Mediodía — vuelta desde Albero Alto
description: This moderate 23.4-mile touring cycling route in Hoya de Huesca explores the Valle del Rio Flumen, passing historical sites and natural land
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Albero Alto traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features routes with moderate to significant elevation gains, often passing through historical areas and open countryside. These paths provide opportunities to explore the local environment, from gentle slopes to more challenging ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Albero Alto The most popular touring cycling route is Albero Alto and Peña Mediodía Loop, a 23.4 miles (37.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a balanced ride through local scenery, including views of Peña Mediodía. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alerre – Huesca loop from Huesca, a difficult 31.5 miles (50.7 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain as it connects the towns of Alerre and Huesca. Local touring cyclists also love the Huesca – Montearagón Castle loop from Huesca, a 41.7 miles (67.2 km) trail leading through historical landscapes towards Montearagón Castle, often completed in about 5 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Albero Alto is defined by rolling hills, agricultural plains, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Albero Alto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Albero Alto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Albero Alto?
A: There are over 85 touring cycling routes around Albero Alto, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Albero Alto?
A: Yes, Albero Alto offers a good selection of routes suitable for beginners and families. There are 20 easy routes and 50 moderate routes available, providing gentler terrain and manageable distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974236696/albero-alto-and-pena-mediodia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albero Alto and Peña Mediodía Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that offers a balanced ride through local scenery.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Albero Alto?
A: The touring cycling routes around Albero Alto traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains. You can expect varied terrain, from gentle slopes to more challenging ascents, with some routes featuring significant elevation gains. The area provides a mix of open countryside and historical areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Albero Alto?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Albero Alto are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974236696/albero-alto-and-pena-mediodia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albero Alto and Peña Mediodía Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca – Montearagón Castle loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Albero Alto often pass by significant historical sites and natural landmarks. You can explore places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5254839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montearagón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arab Wall of Huesca&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca – Montearagón Castle loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to take you through these historical landscapes.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Albero Alto?
A: The touring cycling routes in Albero Alto are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and the mix of moderate to challenging rides.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Albero Alto offers 17 difficult routes. Options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38182187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alerre – Huesca loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt; (31.5 miles with substantial elevation gain) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19886293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca – Vadiello Dam loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt; (over 33 miles with significant climbs) provide demanding rides through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Albero Alto?
A: While specific seasonal data for Albero Alto isn't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in regions with rolling hills and agricultural plains, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential cold/wet conditions of winter. May and June are often cited as popular months for cycling in similar European regions.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes in Albero Alto?
A: The suitability of bringing dogs on touring cycling routes can vary depending on local regulations and the specific trail. While some paths may be dog-friendly, it's always best to check local signage or specific route details on komoot for any restrictions or requirements regarding pets, especially in nature preserves or protected areas.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access the touring cycling routes?
A: Information on specific public transport access points for each route is not detailed in the guide. However, for routes starting or passing through larger towns like Huesca, it's likely that local bus or train services could provide access. We recommend checking local public transport schedules and policies regarding bicycles for the most accurate information.

Q: Where can I find parking near the touring cycling routes in Albero Alto?
A: While specific parking locations are not listed for each route, routes that start or pass through towns such as Albero Alto or Huesca typically have public parking available. For routes in more rural areas, look for designated parking at trailheads or consider starting from a nearby village with amenities.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Albero Alto pass through or near villages and towns, especially those connecting places like Alerre and Huesca, or leading to Montearagón Castle. These locations often provide opportunities to stop for refreshments at local cafes, restaurants, or shops. It's always a good idea to check your komoot route map for points of interest along the way.
tips: The Valdabra Reservoir is located very close to the city of Huesca. Built in the early 1980s, over the years it has become a first-line setting for cyprinid fishing, mainly Carp and Roach. Prestigious championships such as the International Fiestas de San Lorenzo are held there.

In the town of Vicién there are a large number of reminiscences of the Civil War. This is the case with the caves (shelters and gunpowder) and the nests of the puffin in the north of the town.
On the outskirts of the town there is a wall that was part of an old fortification.
There is also the San Gregorio Nacianceno church

Muslim fortification from the 10th century located on a sandstone bank 80 meters long and 25 meters high.
It is worth stopping and climbing to the top to enjoy incredible views of the Hoya de Huesca.

TREES AS ARCHEOLOGY (Art and Nature)
Its author is Fernando Casas.

faqs.question: How much time should I allocate for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The Valle del Rio Flumen y Peña Mediodía — return from Albero Alto route typically takes around 2 hours and 19 minutes to complete for touring cyclists. This is an average, so plan accordingly based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it has a manageable elevation gain of about 235 meters over 37.7 kilometers, the mixed terrain includes paved, asphalt, and some unpaved sections. Beginners with a reasonable fitness level should find it enjoyable, but those new to cycling might prefer a shorter, flatter route first.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Valle del Rio Flumen y Peña Mediodía cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, making for an engaging ride. You'll find approximately 25.8 kilometers of paved roads and 10.3 kilometers of asphalt. There are also shorter unpaved sections, totaling about 1.43 kilometers, which are generally suitable for touring bikes. This mix provides a diverse cycling experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park and start cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The route is named 'Valle del Rio Flumen y Peña Mediodía — return from Albero Alto,' suggesting Albero Alto as a primary starting and return point. You'll likely find suitable parking options within or near the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting spots. Highlights include the scenic Valdabra Reservoir, the historically significant area of Vicién, and the impressive natural monument of Peña Mediodía de Piracés. Additionally, you can see the unique art installation, Trees as Archaeology by Fernando Casas.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Peña Mediodía de Piracés is a significant landmark, an 80-meter long and 25-meter high sandstone bank with remnants of a 10th-century Muslim fortification. It offers a striking visual point along your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily for touring bicycles and includes paved sections, dogs are generally welcome on trails in the region, especially if kept on a leash. However, always ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling speeds and distances, and be mindful of other cyclists and local regulations, particularly in populated areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Hoya de Huesca region?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails in the Hoya de Huesca region, including this route. However, it's always good practice to respect local signage and any specific regulations that might apply to certain protected areas or private lands you might pass through.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Hoya de Huesca region, where this route is located, experiences hot summers. Therefore, the best times to cycle are typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant. Winter can also be pleasant on sunny days, but be prepared for cooler temperatures.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the starting point?
faqs.answer: Albero Alto, being a village, likely offers basic amenities. For a wider range of cafes, pubs, and accommodation options, you might consider nearby larger towns. It's advisable to check local listings for Albero Alto before your trip to confirm specific services available.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with official trails such as BTT 14 Ruta Sillares, trincheras y estepas, BTT 07 Sillares y Trincheras I, and Recorrido por el humedal de Valdabra, among others. This offers opportunities to explore further if you wish to extend your ride.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length of 37.7 kilometers, this route is best suited for families with older children or teenagers who are experienced cyclists. The mixed terrain and duration might be challenging for very young children or those new to cycling long distances.
